title: Add rate limiting to auth endpoints
description:
## Issue
No rate limiting on authentication endpoints allows brute-force attacks.

## Requirements
- Add rate limiting middleware for auth endpoints
- Requires new dependency and design work — track separately

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/routes.rs
- crates/wifisync-server/Cargo.toml

title: CI workflow for E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Create GitHub Actions CI workflow for running E2E tests:

### Workflow File (`.github/workflows/e2e-tests.yml`)
- Trigger: push to main/master, PR to main/master
- Jobs:
  1. **cli-e2e**: Build CLI, start Docker server, run CLI E2E tests
  2. **cross-device-e2e** (optional/nightly): Start Docker server + Android emulator, run full suite

### Environment Setup
- Docker Compose for server
- Rust toolchain for CLI build
- Android SDK + emulator (for cross-device job)
- KVM acceleration for emulator (GitHub Actions supports this on Linux runners)

### Configuration
- Use `./tests/e2e/run-e2e.sh --cli-only` for the CLI-only job
- Use `./tests/e2e/run-e2e.sh` for the full suite job
- Cache Cargo build artifacts and Android SDK
- Timeout: 30 minutes for CLI-only, 60 minutes for full suite

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI E2E tests run on every push/PR to main
- Cross-device E2E tests run on nightly schedule (or manual trigger)
- CI passes when all E2E tests pass
- CI fails fast with clear error output on failure
- Emulator boot and test execution work reliably on GitHub Actions

## Context
- Depends on all other E2E test issues being implemented
- Existing CI workflows in `.github/workflows/`
- Android emulator on CI needs `reactivecircus/android-emulator-runner` action or similar
- Server Docker image built from `docker/` directory

title: Make bcrypt cost configurable
description:
## Issue
Bcrypt uses DEFAULT_COST (12), not configurable. Making it configurable allows tuning per deployment.

## Requirements
- Add BCRYPT_COST env var to ServerConfig
- Default to 12, validate range 4-31

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/handlers/auth.rs:39,70

title: Parallelize independent package builds in build.sh
description:
## Requirements
- Run independent builds concurrently using background processes and wait
- RPM/DEB/Android builds are independent and can run in parallel
- After compile-once change, fan out 4 packaging jobs + Android in parallel
- Properly handle errors from parallel jobs (fail if any child fails)

## Context
- packaging/build.sh build_all() runs everything sequentially
- Docker builds are independent containers
- Android build is independent of Linux packaging

## Acceptance Criteria
- build_all runs packaging jobs in parallel
- Errors from any parallel job cause overall failure
- Output is still collected in dist/

title: Conflict resolution E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Implement conflict resolution E2E tests using two CLI data dirs simulating two devices:

### Tests (in `tests/e2e/cli_e2e.rs`)
- `test_concurrent_update_creates_conflict`: Device A pushes credential. Device B pulls, modifies same credential, pushes. Device A modifies same credential, pushes — Device A's push reports a conflict
- `test_resolve_keep_local`: Create conflict, `sync resolve <id> --keep-local` — local version wins, conflict cleared
- `test_resolve_keep_remote`: Create conflict, `sync resolve <id> --keep-remote` — remote version wins
- `test_resolve_keep_both`: Create conflict, `sync resolve <id> --keep-both` — both versions preserved as separate credentials
- `test_list_conflicts`: Create conflict, `sync conflicts --json` — shows conflict with both versions
- `test_delete_conflict`: Device A deletes credential. Device B updates same credential, pushes. Device A pushes delete — conflict between delete and update

### Multi-device simulation
- Two separate `WIFISYNC_DATA_DIR` directories for Device A and Device B
- Both logged in as same user with different device_ids
- Sequential operations to create deterministic conflicts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conflicts are reliably created and detected
- All resolution strategies (keep-local, keep-remote, keep-both) work correctly
- `sync conflicts --json` output is parseable and complete
- Delete vs update conflicts handled correctly

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- CLI commands: `wifisync sync resolve`, `wifisync sync conflicts`
- Vector clock based conflict detection

title: CLI collection and network management E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Implement CLI-only E2E tests in `tests/e2e/cli_e2e.rs` for collection and network management:

### Collection Management Tests
- `test_create_collection`: Create collection, verify in list output
- `test_create_duplicate_collection`: Create same collection twice, verify idempotent or error
- `test_delete_collection`: Create then delete, verify removed from list
- `test_list_collections`: Create 3 collections, verify all appear in `--json` output
- `test_show_collection`: Create collection with credential, verify in show output

### Network Management Tests
- `test_add_network_to_collection`: Import credential, add to collection, verify
- `test_remove_network_from_collection`: Add then remove network, verify removed
- `test_add_nonexistent_network`: Add non-existent SSID, verify descriptive error
- `test_add_to_nonexistent_collection`: Add to non-existent collection, verify error

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests pass against live Docker server
- Each test uses unique username and temp data dir
- Tests invoke real `wifisync` binary as subprocess (black-box testing)
- JSON output is parsed and validated where applicable

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- CLI commands: `wifisync collection create/delete/list/show/add/remove`
- Uses helpers.rs for CLI invocation

title: Add --parallel flag to Gradle Android build
description:
## Requirements
- Add --parallel flag to Gradle invocation for parallel task execution
- Combine with the single-invocation change

## Context
- packaging/build.sh build_android()
- Gradle --parallel enables concurrent task execution within a single build

## Acceptance Criteria
- Gradle invocation includes --parallel flag
- Build still produces correct debug and release APKs

title: Fix Docker health check to verify HTTP server
description:
## Issue
Current HEALTHCHECK uses 'sqlite3 /data/wifisync.db SELECT 1' which only verifies the database file exists. A hung HTTP server process passes this check.

## Requirements
- Change health check to curl the /health endpoint
- Add curl to the runtime Docker image
- Verify the HTTP server is actually responding

## Files
- Dockerfile:97-98

title: Use Docker BuildKit cache mounts for Cargo directories
description:
## Requirements
- Use BuildKit --mount=type=cache for Cargo registry, git, and target directories
- Apply to both Dockerfile.fedora and Dockerfile.ubuntu
- Requires DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 or Docker Engine 23+

## Context
- packaging/docker/Dockerfile.fedora and Dockerfile.ubuntu
- BuildKit cache mounts are the modern approach for build caching
- Complements volume mounts for runtime docker run caching

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dockerfiles use RUN --mount=type=cache where applicable
- Build scripts set DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 if needed

title: Enforce JWT expiration during token validation
description:
## Issue
Validation::default() in jsonwebtoken crate behavior needs verification. The security review claims it does NOT validate token expiration by default, allowing expired tokens to be decoded before the is_expired() check.

## Requirements
- Verify Validation::default() behavior for exp claim
- If not validated, explicitly set validate_exp = true
- Add test proving expired tokens are rejected at the decode level
- Clean up redundant is_expired() check if decode already handles it

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/auth.rs:63-70
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/auth.rs:106-108

title: Invalid password E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Implement invalid password E2E tests across CLI and Android:

### CLI Tests (in `tests/e2e/cli_e2e.rs`)
- `test_wrong_password_push_rejected`: Login with password A, attempt push with password B — error: 'Password does not match' (auth_proof check)
- `test_wrong_password_pull_rejected`: Login with password A, attempt pull with password B — error: 'Password does not match'
- `test_wrong_password_login`: Register user with password A, logout, login with password B — login fails (bcrypt mismatch on server)

### Android Tests (in `InvalidPasswordTest.kt`, new file)
- `test_wrong_password_android_push`: Login, attempt push with wrong password — JNI returns error JSON
- `test_wrong_password_android_pull`: Login, attempt pull with wrong password — JNI returns error JSON with details

### Cross-device Tests
- `test_cross_device_wrong_password`: CLI pushes with password A. Android logs in same user with password B, pulls — pull fails: decryption error (different encryption key)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wrong password on push/pull produces clear error message (not a crash or panic)
- Auth_proof verification catches password mismatches before server round-trip
- Cross-device encryption key mismatch detected and reported clearly
- bcrypt password mismatch on login returns proper error

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- Recent bug: Android pull '0 applied 2 errors' with wrong password (fixed in 7cd102c)
- Encryption uses password-derived key, auth_proof verifies password consistency

title: Compile once, package twice — build binaries once then fan out to RPM/DEB packaging
description:
## Requirements
- Build both CLI and server binaries in a single cargo build invocation
- Pass pre-built binaries to RPM/DEB packaging containers instead of recompiling
- Restructure entrypoint scripts to accept a pre-built binary path
- Cut 4 Cargo compiles down to 1

## Context
- packaging/build.sh orchestrates all builds
- packaging/docker/rpm-entrypoint.sh and deb-entrypoint.sh run cargo build inside Docker
- packaging/rpm/wifisync.spec and packaging/deb/debian/rules both call cargo build --release
- Same for server variants

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single cargo build produces both wifisync and wifisync-server binaries
- RPM and DEB builds use pre-built binaries, no cargo compilation inside packaging containers
- All 4 packages (cli-rpm, cli-deb, server-rpm, server-deb) still produce correct output

title: CLI push/pull E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Implement CLI push/pull E2E tests in `tests/e2e/cli_e2e.rs` against live Docker server:

### Tests
- `test_push_empty_collection`: Create empty collection, push — no error, server has collection
- `test_pull_no_changes`: Login, push, pull immediately — 0 applied, 0 errors
- `test_push_after_pull`: Push, pull, modify locally, push again — succeeds with incremented vector clock
- `test_first_sync_pushes_all`: Login first time with existing credentials, push — all pushed
- `test_cli_push_pull_roundtrip`: Create collection with networks, push, new data dir, login same user, pull — data matches

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests pass against live Docker server
- Push/pull operations verified via CLI output and local DB state
- Each test uses unique username and isolated data dir
- Tests verify exit codes, stdout messages, and (where possible) JSON output

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- CLI commands: `wifisync sync push`, `wifisync sync pull`
- Recent bugs: duplicate collection 500/400s on push (fixed in 916c293)

title: E2E test infrastructure and orchestrator
description:
## Requirements
- Create `tests/e2e/run-e2e.sh` orchestrator script that:
  - Builds CLI binary (`cargo build --release -p wifisync-cli`)
  - Starts Docker server with fresh volume (clean DB each run)
  - Runs CLI-side Rust integration tests
  - Optionally runs Android instrumented tests
  - Tears down server, optionally keeps emulator
  - Supports flags: `--cli-only`, `--android-only`, `--keep-emulator`
- Create `tests/e2e/docker-compose.e2e.yml` with fresh volume, port 8080
- Create `tests/e2e/helpers.rs` with shared helpers:
  - CLI invocation via `std::process::Command`
  - Temp data dir creation per test
  - Unique username generation (`test_<uuid>`)
  - JSON output parsing
  - Server readiness check
- Wire up Cargo workspace for E2E test binary

## Acceptance Criteria
- `./tests/e2e/run-e2e.sh` starts server, runs a smoke test, and tears down cleanly
- `./tests/e2e/run-e2e.sh --cli-only` skips Android tests
- helpers.rs provides reusable CLI invocation that captures stdout/stderr/exit code
- Each test gets isolated data dir and unique username

## Context
- Server Docker setup exists in `docker/` directory
- CLI binary is `wifisync-cli` crate
- Android emulator connects to server via `10.0.2.2:8080`
- This issue blocks all other E2E test issues

title: JWT expiry and token refresh E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Implement JWT expiry and token refresh E2E tests:

### Server Config Change
- Add `JWT_EXPIRATION_SECONDS` env var to server config (`crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs`)
- When set, overrides `JWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS` for fine-grained testing
- Start a second server instance with very short expiry (1 second) via docker-compose override

### Tests (in `tests/e2e/jwt_e2e.rs`, new file)
- `test_token_refresh_on_push`: Login, wait for token to expire, push — succeeds (auto-refresh)
- `test_token_refresh_on_pull`: Login, wait for token to expire, pull — succeeds
- `test_expired_token_status`: Login, wait for expiry, check status — shows `hasValidToken: false`
- `test_manual_token_refresh`: Login, let token expire, call refresh — new token issued
- `test_invalid_token_rejected`: Manually corrupt token in config, attempt push — 401 error, graceful handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- Server supports `JWT_EXPIRATION_SECONDS` env var for testing
- Tests reliably trigger token expiry without flaky timing
- Auto-refresh works transparently on push/pull
- Invalid/corrupt tokens produce clear error messages

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- Requires docker-compose override for short-expiry server
- JWT handling in `crates/wifisync-server/src/` auth module

title: Login/logout E2E tests
description:
## Requirements
Implement login/logout E2E tests across CLI and Android:

### CLI Tests (in `tests/e2e/cli_e2e.rs`)
- `test_cli_login_logout`: Login, verify status shows connected, logout — status shows disconnected, config deleted
- `test_login_stores_auth_proof`: Login, check sync_config.json — `auth_proof` field is set
- `test_logout_clears_token`: Login, logout, check sync_config.json — config file deleted
- `test_relogin_same_user`: Login, logout, login again — works, new device_id assigned
- `test_login_new_user_registration`: Login with fresh username — user auto-registered, login succeeds
- `test_multiple_devices_same_user`: CLI logs in, second CLI data dir logs in same user — both have distinct device_ids, both can push/pull

### Android Tests (in `LiveSyncE2eTest.kt`)
- `test_android_login_logout`: Login via JNI, check status, logout — status shows disabled after logout

## Acceptance Criteria
- Login creates proper config files with auth_proof and device_id
- Logout cleanly removes config/token
- Re-login works after logout
- Fresh username triggers auto-registration on server
- Multiple devices can coexist on same user account

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- Config stored in `sync_config.json` in data dir
- Recent bug: server login panic on legacy user re-registration (fixed in 2130442)

title: Mount Cargo cache volumes in Docker packaging builds
description:
## Requirements
- Mount persistent Docker volumes for ~/.cargo/registry and ~/.cargo/git
- Second+ builds only recompile changed code, not all dependencies
- Add volume mounts to all 4 docker run invocations in build-rpm.sh, build-deb.sh, build-rpm-server.sh, build-deb-server.sh

## Context
- Each Docker run starts with empty Cargo cache
- Every dependency is re-downloaded and re-compiled from scratch
- Named volumes persist between runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docker run commands include -v cargo-registry:/root/.cargo/registry -v cargo-git:/root/.cargo/git
- Subsequent builds are significantly faster due to cached dependencies

title: Combine Gradle assembleDebug and assembleRelease into single invocation
description:
## Requirements
- Combine two separate Gradle invocations into one
- Remove --no-daemon flag to allow daemon reuse
- Change from:
  ./gradlew assembleDebug --no-daemon --no-configuration-cache
  ./gradlew assembleRelease --no-daemon --no-configuration-cache
  To:
  ./gradlew assembleDebug assembleRelease --no-configuration-cache

## Context
- packaging/build.sh build_android() lines 227-230
- Each --no-daemon invocation has JVM startup overhead

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single Gradle invocation builds both debug and release APKs
- Both APKs still copied to dist/ correctly

title: Cross-device push/pull E2E tests (CLI + Android)
description:
## Requirements
Implement cross-device E2E tests that exercise CLI and Android together:

### CLI-side (in `tests/e2e/cli_e2e.rs`)
- CLI prepares data (login, create collections, add networks, push)
- CLI verifies data after Android push (login same user, pull, check)

### Android-side (new `LiveSyncE2eTest.kt`)
- `test_cli_push_android_pull`: CLI pushes 2 networks in a collection. Android logs in same user, pulls — sees both networks in correct collection
- `test_android_push_cli_pull`: Android creates collection, adds network, pushes. CLI pulls — sees the network
- `test_pull_creates_missing_collections`: CLI pushes collections. Android logs in fresh, pulls — creates local collections it didn't have

### Orchestration
- `run-e2e.sh` coordinates: CLI phase first, then Android phase (linear)
- Pass username/password/server URL to Android via `testInstrumentationRunnerArguments`
- Android emulator connects to `http://10.0.2.2:8080`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Data pushed by CLI is correctly pulled by Android and vice versa
- Collections, credentials, and SSIDs match across devices
- Orchestrator script handles coordination without race conditions

## Context
- Depends on E2E infrastructure (Wifisync-kzu)
- Existing pattern: `LiveSyncTestConfig.kt` uses `InstrumentationRegistry.getArguments()`
- Android instrumented tests run via `./gradlew connectedAndroidTest`

title: Enable SQLite WAL mode for better concurrency
description:
## Issue
SQLite not using WAL mode. WAL improves concurrent read performance for multi-client sync server.

## Requirements
- Enable WAL mode via PRAGMA after database connection
- Add test

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/db/mod.rs:12-15

title: Make CORS configuration restrictive by default
description:
## Issue
CORS allows all origins, methods, and headers. While this server is primarily for mobile/CLI (where CORS is irrelevant), it's good practice to restrict.

## Requirements
- Make CORS origins configurable via CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var
- Restrict allowed methods to GET, POST, DELETE
- Restrict allowed headers to Content-Type and Authorization

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/routes.rs:18-21
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs

title: Reject empty JWT_SECRET environment variable
description:
## Issue
If JWT_SECRET="" (empty string) is explicitly set, env::var() succeeds and returns empty string. An empty secret makes JWTs trivially forgeable since anyone can sign tokens with an empty key.

## Requirements
- Treat empty JWT_SECRET same as unset (generate random or reject)
- Add validation that panics/errors on startup if JWT_SECRET is empty in production
- Add test

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs:31-40
